mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL erodes real equity returns and forces tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of CPIAUCSL uptick instances.

mechanism_narrative: UNRATE crossing above 5.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increase in RSAFS and decrease in FEDFUNDS indicates accommodative monetary policy boosting consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to produce positive returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings revisions, driving positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of recovery-phase INDPRO upticks, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: HOUST falling below 1.1 million units signals a housing market collapse that drags down construction spending and financial sector balance sheets, producing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in >60% of triggered observations, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining FEDFUNDS reduces the discount rate for equity valuations and lowers corporate borrowing costs, mechanically lifting S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.05 over any 2-year window, or positive returns dominate during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising UNRATE combined with falling PAYEMS confirms broad-based job losses that suppress consumer spending forecasts, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive SPX returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: GDPC1 growth exceeding 2 percent annualized during a late-cycle expansion indicates resilient aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of instances when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2 percent in this regime, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.
